# IntentText (.it) — Format Reference for LLMs

You are reading the canonical machine reference for IntentText, an open plain-text
document format. After reading this file you can author, edit, and reason about `.it`
files correctly. One `.it` file is simultaneously: a human-readable document, a
queryable typed database, a print/PDF-ready artifact, and a tamper-evident signable
record.

CORE PRINCIPLE — OPEN VOCABULARY: .it has NO fixed schema. The 41 reserved keywords (§2)
have stable, guaranteed meaning, but they are NOT a whitelist. Inventing your own domain
keywords — clause:, risk:, obligation:, sla:, warranty:, expense: — is normal,
first-class, and ENCOURAGED whenever no reserved keyword fits. Custom keywords parse, render,
seal, and query exactly like reserved ones (§2c). A real document freely MIXES reserved and
custom keywords; do NOT restrict yourself to the reserved set — that makes documents poorer.
ZERO ALIASES: the ONLY reserved words are the 41 canonical keywords (§2), their Arabic localized
names (§12), and the namespaced extension keywords (§2b). There are NO synonym aliases — so
party:, milestone:, status:, note:, item:, requirement:, due:, rule:, kpi: and any other word
you invent resolve RELIABLY to your own custom block, never silently reinterpreted as something else.

Site: https://dotit.uts.qa · npm: @dotit/core · Editor: https://editor.uts.qa
Verify portal: https://verify.uts.qa (UTS certification service: api.uts.qa)

## 0. The `@dotit/*` toolchain (one ecosystem)

  @dotit/core   2.0.0  Parser, HTML/print renderers, query engine, template merge (+ Intl
                       display filters {{amount|currency:QAR}}), trust — versioned
                       SEAL_SPEC 4 (seal/sign/verify; appearance hash, CRLF-stable,
                       certify-as-claim), themes, lossless text↔JSON, DB-safe storage,
                       converters (Markdown↔IntentText incl. it→md, HTML→it, XLSX/DOCX both ways), doc-metadata
                       extraction, EN 16931/UBL e-invoice export, and the `dotit` CLI.
                       Zero deps; Node + browser.         npm i @dotit/core
  @dotit/sign   2.0.0  Ed25519 signatures (provable "who signed") + UTS certifications
                       (verified org identity). Offline, self-verifying.  npm i @dotit/sign
  @dotit/pdf    2.0.0  Server PDF bytes: merge → seal → real PDF in one call (issuePDF);
                       PDF/A-oriented archival; PAdES-signed PDF. Puppeteer optional peer.
                                                          npm i @dotit/pdf
  @dotit/pades  1.0.1  PAdES (Adobe/court-recognized) PDF signatures — X.509/CMS +
                       RFC-3161 trusted timestamps.       npm i @dotit/pades
  @dotit/mcp    2.0.0  MCP server — exposes IntentText as an agent tool surface
                       (parse/render/merge/query/validate/seal/verify/sign tools)
                       over stdio + HTTP.                npx @dotit/mcp
  @dotit/editor 2.0.0  Embeddable React WYSIWYG editor — Word-like pages, ribbon,
                       trust stamp, WYSIWYG PDF export. Browser-only.  npm i @dotit/editor

Everything is built on the SAME parser/hash, so a doc authored anywhere verifies everywhere.

## 1. Line grammar — everything is one line, one intent

A non-empty line is one of two things:
  (a) PLAIN PROSE — a bare line of body text (type `text`). You do NOT need `text:`
      for ordinary paragraphs; just write the sentence. This is the clean, preferred
      style for narrative prose.
  (b) A TYPED BLOCK — `keyword: content | prop: value | prop: value`.

- A line is a typed block ONLY when it starts with a single keyword-token — a word of
  letters/hyphens (any language), no spaces, beginning with a letter — immediately
  followed by `:`. That token is the keyword (block type); content is everything after
  the `:` up to the first ` | `. Everything else is prose. So `Pay by: cash`,
  `Ratio is 3:2`, `See https://x.io for docs`, and any line with a mid-sentence colon
  are TEXT, but `Note: …` (one leading token) becomes a custom `Note` block.
- Two gotchas when writing prose bare:
    · A line that starts `Word:` is read as a keyword. To force literal prose that
      starts that way, prefix it: `text: Note: remember the deadline`.
    · A bare URL alone on a line (`https://…`) parses as a custom `https` block — use
      `link: Label | to: https://…`, or keep the URL inline within a sentence.
- Pipe properties are `key: value` pairs separated by ` | `. The `|` character is
  RESERVED as the property delimiter — it cannot appear inside content or values.
  Every OTHER character is literal in content: Unicode punctuation (– × ≤ β ΔE),
  symbols, accents, and RTL/Arabic text need no escaping. Only `|` is special.
- Blank lines separate blocks. They ARE significant between consecutive prose lines
  (bare or `text:`): without a blank line, adjacent prose lines merge into one
  paragraph and only the FIRST line's pipe properties are kept. Put a blank line
  between paragraphs that each carry their own properties.
- `// comment` lines are comments.
- No indentation or nesting rules — blocks are a FLAT sequence; hierarchy comes from
  document order plus `section:`/`sub:` headings, never from leading whitespace.
- Unknown keywords NEVER error — they parse as type:"custom" with the keyword
  preserved (`computer: mac | ram: 16GB` is valid and queryable). This means you can —
  and SHOULD — invent precise domain keywords freely; it is idiomatic, not a workaround.
- Keywords and property keys are UNICODE words — Arabic (or any language) domain
  keywords work exactly like ASCII ones: `مصروف: كراسي مكتب | المورد: ايكيا | فئة: أثاث`
  is a typed, queryable block. Dates stay ISO (2026-03-09) regardless of language.
- `x-ns:` prefixed keywords are namespaced extensions (e.g. `x-layout: divider`).

## 2. The 41 canonical keywords, by tier

CORE tier (13 — a plain document needs only these):
  title:    Document title (one per doc).            title: Service Agreement
  summary:  Subtitle / abstract.                     summary: Q2 consulting
  meta:     Document metadata (props only).          meta: | author: A | tags: x, y | theme: corporate | dir: rtl
            Beyond author/tags/theme/dir, ANY custom key is allowed and preserved
            (e.g. type, ref, client, sponsor, prepared-for, prepared-by, date).
  section:  H2-level heading.                        section: Scope
  sub:      H3-level heading.                        sub: Payment Terms
  text:     Body paragraph — OPTIONAL: a bare line is already text. Use text: only
            when the paragraph needs pipe-props/styling.   text: Net 30 | end: 2026-06-12
  info:     Callout. type: tip|info|warning|danger|success.  info: Check this | type: warning
  quote:    Quotation.                               quote: Stay hungry | by: Jobs
  code:     Code. Prefer fenced: ```lang … ```
  image:    Image.                                   image: Alt text | src: logo.png | caption: Our logo
  link:     Hyperlink block.                         link: Docs | to: https://example.com
  task:     Open task.                               task: Ship auth | owner: Ahmed | due: 2026-07-01 | priority: high
  done:     Completed item.                          done: Deploy staging | at: 2026-03-01
  (lists)   `- item` bullets → type `list-item`; `1. item` ordered lines → type
            `step-item` (a numbered list). Both render as lists; when QUERYING list
            content, match BOTH `list-item` and `step-item`.
  (tables)  Pipe rows: `| Cell | Cell |` — first row = header. Or `headers:`/`row:` keywords.

DATA tier (3):
  metric:   KPI or document total.                   metric: Total Due | value: 17325 | unit: QAR
            Plain metric renders as a label→value total row (invoice style); with
            ANY ONE of target:/trend:/period: it renders as a KPI card. trend: must be one
            of: up | down | stable | at-risk (other words fail strict). MONEY: put the
            bare magnitude in value: and the ISO-4217 code in unit: (queryable, typed);
            the inline "17,325 QAR" form also parses but is less machine-friendly.
  headers:, row:  Tabular data. `headers:` is the table-header keyword (`columns:` is NOT
            reserved anymore — it resolves to a custom block; write `headers:`).

COMMON x-doc EXTENSION BLOCKS (everyday, but NOT among the 41 — namespaced, see §2b):
  def:      Definition/glossary term.                def: Force Majeure | meaning: Events beyond control
  contact:  Person/org card.                         contact: Acme LLC | email: a@b.c | vat: VAT-300
  deadline: Dated obligation.                        deadline: Payment due | date: 2026-03-20 | consequence: late fee
  ref:      Cross-document reference (target in file: or url:, NOT to:).  ref: QT-104 | file: quotations/qt-104.it

CONTRACT (trust) tier (9):
  cite:     Citation / authority reference.          cite: ISO 27001:2022 | section: A.9
  track:    Start version tracking.                  track: | id: DOC-1 | by: Ahmed
  approve:  Named approval (HASHED with body).       approve: Legal ok | by: Sara | role: Counsel | at: 2026-03-28
  sign:     Signature. A CONFORMANT sign: carries hash: + at: — the hash is added when you
            SEAL (key:+sig: add Ed25519 proof). For recorded pre-seal sign-offs use approve:;
            for a blank printed signature LINE use signline:. A bare `sign: Name` with no hash:
            parses but is flagged by checkConformance (SIGN_NO_HASH) — it is not a signature yet.
            sign: Ahmed | role: CEO | at: … | hash: sha256:… | key: ed25519:<pub> | sig: <sig>
  freeze:   Seal the document (locks content).       freeze: | at: … | hash: sha256:… | status: locked
  amendment: Additive change to a sealed doc.        amendment: Terms updated | was: Net 30 | now: Net 15 | by: Ahmed
  certify:  Authority certification claim (see §6 Layer 3).  certify: UTS | account: … | entity: …
  route:    In-file approval policy (see §6c).        route: sequential          (or parallel)
  require:  A required approver (see §6c).            require: finance | when: amount > 100000
  (Below the `history:` boundary — machine-managed, NOT tier members:)
  history:  Boundary line — everything below is the append-only audit log (never hashed).
  revision: Audit-log entry (below history:).

AGENT tier (9 — workflows for AI agents):
  step:     Workflow step.                           step: Validate input | tool: validate | id: s1
  decision: Branch.                                  decision: Valid? | then: s2 | else: s9
  gate:     Human checkpoint.                        gate: Manager approval | approver: Ops Lead
  trigger:, result:, policy:, audit:, ask:, context:  — agent runtime keywords.

PRINT (layout) tier (7):
  page:     Page setup.                              page: | size: A3 | orientation: landscape
            size: A5|A4|A3|A2|A1|Letter|Legal or custom "80mm auto" (receipt roll, no pagination).
            orientation: portrait (default) | landscape — landscape swaps width/height.
            Shorthand: size: A3 landscape. Print/PDF emits the TRUE physical size.
  header:   Running page header.                     header: ACME — Confidential
  footer:   Running page footer.                     footer: INV-1 · Page {{page}} of {{pages}}
  watermark: Watermark overlay.                      watermark: DRAFT | opacity: 0.1
  style:    Scoped document style rule (see §4).
  break:    Forced page break.                       break:
  toc:      Table of contents.

## 2b. Extensions — namespaced keywords (x-ns:)

Beyond the 41 reserved keywords, specialized blocks use an `x-<namespace>:` prefix (the bare
form is also accepted); they parse, render, and query like any block.
  x-writer:  byline figure caption footnote epigraph dedication      (editorial)
  x-doc:     def contact deadline ref signline attach                (definitions, parties, cross-refs, attachments)
  x-agent:   loop parallel retry wait handoff call checkpoint signal import export progress tool prompt memory model error
  x-form:    input output                                            (typed form fields — STABLE namespace)
  x-layout:  font divider                                            (font face; horizontal rule)
  x-exp:     assert secret                                           (experimental)
Examples:
  contact: Acme Gulf Trading WLL | email: ops@acme.qa | role: Client
  deadline: First invoice due | date: 2026-07-01 | consequence: 2% late fee
  def: Force Majeure | meaning: events beyond a party's reasonable control
  ref: Prior policy | file: ./policy.it | rel: supersedes            (ref TARGET goes in file: or url:, NOT to:)
  figure: Chart 1 | src: figures/q2-trend.svg | caption: Q2 revenue trend   (numbered captioned figure — src: is REQUIRED; not an alias for image:)
  attach: cr | name: cr.pdf | mime: application/pdf | sha256: … | data: <base64>   (the file rides INSIDE the doc and is covered by the seal)

## 2c. Custom keywords — the OPEN VOCABULARY (a first-class feature — use it freely)

The 41 reserved keywords are NOT a whitelist. ANY word: that is not reserved parses as a typed,
queryable `custom` block that keeps its keyword verbatim (case + script preserved). Inventing a
precise domain keyword is IDIOMATIC and ENCOURAGED — reach for one whenever no reserved keyword
fits, instead of flattening the concept into text:/info:. All of these are first-class blocks:
  clause: Indemnity | scope: Provider | cap: 3 months fees         (contracts / legal)
  obligation: Deliver Schedule A | owner: Provider | by: 2026-08-01
  risk: Vendor lock-in | severity: high | mitigation: dual-source  (reports / assessments)
  warranty: Conforms to spec | owner: Provider | period: 90 days
  sla: Resolution time | target: 4h | priority: P1
  skill: summarize-contract | input: file | output: brief | model: claude-opus-4-8   (agent skills)
  expense: Office chairs | category: furniture | amount: 1200 | unit: QAR
Query a custom keyword by NAME — `keyword=clause` — NOT `type=clause`: every custom block's `type`
is literally `custom` (the word lives in properties.keyword), so `type=custom` matches them ALL and
`keyword=clause` matches one kind. NO ALIAS COLLISIONS: there are no synonym aliases, so a word is
custom UNLESS it is one of the 41 canonical (§2), an Arabic localized name (§12), or a namespaced
extension keyword (§2b: def, contact, deadline, ref, signline, attach, figure, byline, input, …).
That means party:, milestone:, rule:, status:, note:, item:, due:, kpi: are all ordinary custom
blocks — never silently reinterpreted. Everything you invent is yours: unknown keywords NEVER
error, round-trip byte-for-byte, and seal/verify like any block — mixing reserved + custom in ONE
document is normal.

## 2d. Format version stamp (optional)

Declare the grammar a file targets with ONE header comment. It is a comment, so it never affects
the seal hash and round-trips as trivia; parsed into document.version.
  // it-format: 1.0

## 3. Inline formatting (inside content)

  *bold*  _italic_  ~strike~  ^highlight^  `code`  [text](https://url)  [[side note]]
  (Markdown-style **bold**/__bold__ and ~~strike~~ are also accepted and render the same.)
  @mention  #tag  @2026-05-01 (date)  {Label} (badge pill)

Styled span — style PART of a line with the same style keys as block props, but
SEMICOLON-separated (| is reserved for line props):
  text: Payment is [overdue]{ color: #c00; weight: bold } — act now.
  metric: Total | value: [17,325 QAR]{ size: 1.2em; weight: bold }

## 4. Styling — three layers (never write raw CSS)

1. Theme (document class): meta: | theme: corporate|legal|editorial|technical|minimal|warm|dark|print
2. style: rules — house styling per block type, declared once, document-wide:
     style: section | color: #0a7 | weight: 600
     style: title | family: Georgia | size: 26pt
   Targets: title summary section sub text quote callout info table table-header
   metric contact divider. Rules apply after the theme; per-line props win over rules.
3. Per-line style props and [text]{…} spans for exceptions.

Style property keys (the ONLY style vocabulary — never arbitrary CSS):
  color, size, family, weight, align(center|right|justify), bg, indent, opacity,
  italic: true, border: true, underline: true, strike: true, valign(sub|super)

## 5. Templates and merge

Placeholders: {{path.to.value}} (dot paths, array indices: {{items.0.qty}}).
Repeating table rows — add `each: <arrayPath>` to the header row; the next row is the
item template, with each element bound to `item`:

  | Description | Qty | Total | each: items |
  | {{item.description}} | {{item.qty}} | {{item.total}} |

Runtime print tokens: {{page}} and {{pages}} in header:/footer: become live page
numbers in print and in the editor. {{date}} {{year}} resolve automatically.

API: parseAndMerge(templateSource, data, { missing: "blank" }) — "blank" renders
unresolved fields empty (finished documents); default "keep" shows the marker
(authoring). Never hand-interpolate; use the merge engine.

Complete invoice template:

  page: | size: A4
  header: {{company.name}}
  footer: {{invoice.number}} · Page {{page}} of {{pages}}

  title: Invoice {{invoice.number}}
  summary: {{company.name}} → {{customer.name}}
  meta: | date: {{invoice.date}} | status: {{invoice.status}}

  section: Bill To
  contact: {{customer.name}} | email: {{customer.email}}

  section: Line Items
  | Description | Qty | Unit Price | Total | each: items |
  | {{item.description}} | {{item.qty}} | {{item.unitPrice}} | {{item.total}} |

  section: Totals
  metric: Subtotal | value: {{totals.subtotal}}
  metric: Tax ({{totals.taxRate}}%) | value: {{totals.tax}}
  metric: Total Due | value: {{totals.due}}

## 6. Trust — three layers: integrity → identity → authority

Build trust up, layer by layer. Each layer is opt-in and verifiable offline.

LAYER 1 — INTEGRITY (seal/freeze): proves the bytes have not changed.
  The document hash = SHA-256 over the source ABOVE the `history:` boundary, EXCLUDING
  lines starting with sign:/freeze:/certify:/amendment: (approve: IS included), joined
  with LF, trimmed, UTF-8. Anyone can recompute it — no vendor needed.
  @dotit/core:
    sealDocument(source, { signer, role? }) → { success, hash, source, at }
    verifyDocument(source) → { intact, frozen, signers, hash, expectedHash }
    computeDocumentHash(source) → "sha256:…"   isSealed(doc) / isSignedBy(doc, name)
  freeze: | at: … | hash: sha256:… | status: locked   ← the seal line.
  Proves: nobody altered the sealed content. Says NOTHING about who froze it.

LAYER 2 — IDENTITY (Ed25519 sign:): proves a specific key-holder signed THIS hash.
  @dotit/sign:
    generateSigningKey() → { privateKey, publicKey }   (base64url, raw 32-byte keys)
    signDocumentCrypto(source, { signer, role, privateKey }) → { success, source }
    verifyCryptoSignatures(source) → [{ signer, role, at, publicKey, cryptographic, valid }]
  Line format (exact):
    sign: Name | role: … | at: … | hash: sha256:… | key: ed25519:<pub> | sig: <sig>
  A conformant `sign:` carries hash: (added by sealing) + at:; signDocumentCrypto() then adds
  key:+sig:. A bare `sign: Name` (no hash:) parses but is NOT a conformant signature — for
  unsigned, pre-seal sign-offs use approve: (a recorded named sign-off, included in the hash).
  HONEST SCOPE: a valid signature proves "the holder of public key <pub> signed this exact
  hash" — it does NOT by itself prove the human's real-world identity. That is Layer 3.

LAYER 3 — AUTHORITY (UTS certify:): binds the signature to a verified org identity.
  Issued by the UTS certification service (api.uts.qa) after it verifies the account/entity,
  then re-checkable at https://verify.uts.qa. @dotit/sign:
    certifyDocument(source, { issuer, issuerPrivateKey, account, entity }) → { source, … }
    verifyCertifications(source) → [{ issuer, account, entity, publicKey, signatureValid,
                                      trusted, valid }]   (trusted = issuer in trusted set)
  Line format (exact):
    certify: UTS | account: … | entity: … | at: … | hash: sha256:… | key: ed25519:… | sig: …
  Proves: an authority (UTS) attests the signing entity is who they claim. This is the
  layer that turns "someone holding a key" into "Al-Diwan Contracting W.L.L., verified".

Lifecycle: track → approve → sign → freeze (seal) → [crypto sign] → [certify] → verify → amendment.
HOW AN AGENT VERIFIES: never hand-compute or invent a hash/sig. Call verifyDocument()
(integrity), verifyCryptoSignatures() (identity), verifyCertifications() (authority), or
GET verify.uts.qa for the public report. Only the libs/service compute valid hashes.
CRITICAL: store sealed source as returned. SEAL_SPEC 4 tolerates CRLF / trailing-whitespace
normalization and excludes styling, but ANY content change breaks the hash. Never edit
above history: after sealing; use amendment: instead.

STORING .it IN A DATABASE: a .it file is just a UTF-8 string — store it in any TEXT/blob
column, string in, string out. To guarantee a DB layer (encoding, trimming, CRLF) can't
silently corrupt sealed bytes, wrap it:
  toStorageRecord(source) → { source, bytesSha256 }   ← persist this record
  fromStorageRecord(record) → source                  ← byte-exact restore
  verifyStorageRecord(record) → boolean                ← bytes survived the round-trip
documentToSource(parseIntentText(src)) is a lossless text↔JSON round-trip for indexing/diff
without touching the stored bytes.

## 6b. Forms — fillable, conditional, computed, signable

A form is `meta: | type: form` + `input:` fields. Two-party trust: the AUTHOR seals the blank
form's structure; the FILLER signs the answers.
  input: Full name | key: name | type: text | required: yes
  input: Country | key: country | type: choice | options: SA, QA, AE   (type: text|choice|checkbox|signature|number|date — choice renders the options:)
  input: VAT number | key: vat | show-if: country == SA            (conditional — shows/counts only when true)
  input: Qty | key: qty | type: number
  input: Total | key: total | type: number | compute: qty * unit_price   (derived; the filler never types it)
  input: CR (PDF) | key: cr | type: attachment | required: yes
`show-if:` and `require:`-`when:` take ONE comparison `key <op> value`, ops == != > < >= <= (= is
loose ==; numeric when both sides parse). `compute:` is arithmetic (+ - * / with parentheses).
A COMPLETE form (all required filled) becomes a signable record. APIs: submitForm() / buildSubmission().

## 6c. Workflows & approval routing — state DERIVED from the file (never stored)

A document carries its own approval policy; live state is derived, so it can never drift from a DB:
  route: sequential                          (or `parallel`)
  require: manager
  require: finance | when: amount > 100000    (conditional — evaluated against the doc's own metric:/meta values)
  require: legal
  approve: Reviewed | by: Sarah | role: manager | at: 2026-03-20    (fulfils require: manager)
The approve: `role:` (or `by:`) MUST match the require: token — `require: legal` is fulfilled by
`role: legal`, NOT by `role: Legal Counsel`. Keep the require token and the approver role identical.
workflowState(source) → { pending, next, complete }; the renderer draws an approval-route panel.
EXECUTABLE agent workflows use the agent tier: trigger: (entry) → step: (run a tool) → decision:
(branch, `if:` condition) → gate: (human checkpoint) → result: (terminal). executeWorkflow() runs
them; `audit:` lines + the hash-chained audit trail (appendApproval / verifyAuditChain) make the
approval SEQUENCE tamper-evident (no insert/delete/reorder without detection).

## 6d. Conformance — one read-only yes/no

  checkConformance(source, { level: "lax" | "strict" }) → { conformant, errors, warnings, issues }
  lax (default): no error-level issues. Unknown keywords are NOT errors (the open vocabulary is conformant by design).
  strict: no errors AND no warnings — the level a publisher certifies for a spotless document.
  Common strict-only warnings (none of these block lax): non-ISO dates; metric `trend:`
  outside up|down|stable|at-risk; `policy:` with no condition (if:/always:/never:); `gate:`
  with no approver:; `figure:` with no caption:; `sign:` with no hash:.

## 7. Rendering and PDFs

  renderHTML(doc, { theme })   → HTML fragment (<div class="intent-document">…)
  renderPrint(doc, { theme })  → complete print-ready HTML document: @page
                                 size/margins, running header/footer with page
                                 counters, multi-page tables
Browser: print renderPrint output via an iframe → native dialog → PDF (zero deps).
Server PDFs (real bytes, for email/archive): npm i @dotit/pdf →
  issuePDF(template, data, { signer }) → { source (sealed .it), hash, at, pdf (bytes) }
  issueDocument(...) / renderPDF(html) / htmlToPDF(html) for lower-level control.
One call does merge → seal → PDF, so the archived PDF and the sealed .it share one hash.

## 8. Query — a folder of .it files is a database

  queryBlocks(doc, "type=task owner=Ahmed sort:due:asc limit:5")
      → { blocks: [...], total, matched }   (results are in .blocks)
  queryDocument / queryComposed (across many docs) / parseQuery(q) → { where, sort, limit, offset }
  CLI: dotit query ./docs "type=invoice status=Unpaid amount>1000 sort:due:asc limit:20"
WHERE clauses use `=` (also `<` `>` `<=` `>=` `!=`, date-aware on ISO values); match any
keyword or property (type=, owner=, status=, due=…); a CUSTOM keyword matches by `keyword=<word>`
(its block type is `custom`, so `type=clause` finds nothing — use `keyword=clause`). SORT uses `sort:field:asc|desc`;
paging uses `limit:N` / `offset:N`. Folder indexing is shallow, per-folder (.it-index),
self-healing on query — no DB server. buildShallowIndex/composeIndexes build the index;
askDocuments runs a natural-language-style ask over a folder.

## 9. Big / multi-page documents

  page: | size: A4 | margin: 20mm   sizes: A5|A4|A3|A2|A1|Letter|Legal, or custom "80mm auto".
                                    orientation: portrait|landscape (e.g. size: A3 | orientation: landscape,
                                    or shorthand size: A3 landscape) — big reports/tables print at true size.
  header: / footer: are RUNNING (repeat on every printed page). Page counters:
    footer: REP-2026 · Page {{page}} of {{pages}}   ({{page}}/{{pages}} are live in print + editor)
  break:        force a page break.
  toc:          renders a table of contents from section:/sub: headings.
  Tables auto-repeat their HEADER row across page breaks in renderPrint output.
  Long docs: drive renderPrint(doc,{theme}) → print to PDF (browser) or @dotit/pdf (server).

## 10. Enterprise document patterns (recipes)

These are the load-bearing shapes for real business docs. All parse + validate clean
against core 1.25. Mix freely; invent domain keywords where no canonical one fits.

TENDER + BILL OF QUANTITIES (multi-section costed tables, per-section subtotal, grand total):

  page: | size: A4 | margin: 18mm
  header: Al-Diwan Contracting W.L.L. — CONFIDENTIAL
  footer: Tender TND-2026-014 · Page {{page}} of {{pages}}

  title: Tender Submission — Doha Tower Fit-Out
  meta: | type: tender | ref: TND-2026-014 | client: Qatar Holding | due: 2026-07-15

  section: 1. Bill of Quantities
  sub: 1.1 Civil & Finishes
  | Item | Description | Unit | Qty | Rate (QAR) | Amount (QAR) |
  | 1.1.1 | Gypsum partition, 100mm | m² | 980 | 165 | 161,700 |
  | 1.1.2 | Porcelain floor tiling | m² | 1,420 | 220 | 312,400 |
  metric: Subtotal — Civil & Finishes | value: 474,100 QAR
  sub: 1.2 MEP
  | Item | Description | Unit | Qty | Rate (QAR) | Amount (QAR) |
  | 1.2.1 | VRF air-conditioning | set | 8 | 38,500 | 308,000 |
  metric: Subtotal — MEP | value: 308,000 QAR

  section: 2. Commercial Summary
  metric: Civil & Finishes | value: 474,100 QAR
  metric: MEP | value: 308,000 QAR
  metric: Grand Total (excl. VAT) | value: 782,100 QAR

  section: 3. Authorization
  approve: Commercial review complete | by: Finance Director | role: Finance | at: 2026-07-12
  approve: Executed for the company | by: Fahad Al-Thani | role: Managing Director | at: 2026-07-14
  (full example: examples/tender-boq.it — each BOQ section is its own table + a metric: subtotal,
   then a Commercial Summary of those subtotals + a Grand Total metric.)

FEASIBILITY STUDY (sections, KPI metrics, assumptions, recommendation):

  title: Feasibility Study — 50 MW Solar Plant
  summary: Al Kharsaah · NPV / IRR / payback assessment
  meta: | type: feasibility | ref: FS-2026-02
  section: Executive Summary
  text: The project clears the hurdle rate and is recommended for detailed design.
  section: Key Metrics
  metric: NPV (10y) | value: 142M QAR | trend: up | period: 10 years
  metric: IRR | value: 14.2% | target: 12%
  metric: Payback | value: 6.4 years
  section: Assumptions
  - Capex 380M QAR at 0.9 QAR/Wp
  - Tariff 0.18 QAR/kWh fixed 20y
  section: Recommendation
  info: Proceed to detailed design. | type: success
  (metric: with any one of target:/trend:/period: renders a KPI card; plain metric: is a total row.)

LEGAL / CONTRACT (definitions, parties, obligations, execution + seal):

  title: Service Agreement
  meta: | type: contract | ref: SA-2026-007
  section: 1. Definitions
  def: Services | meaning: The consulting work described in Schedule A
  def: Confidential Information | meaning: Non-public data disclosed by either party
  section: 2. Parties
  contact: Acme LLC | role: Provider | email: legal@acme.qa | vat: VAT-300123
  contact: Globex W.L.L. | role: Client | email: ops@globex.qa
  section: 3. Terms
  text: Payment is Net 30 from invoice date.
  obligation: Deliver Schedule A in full | owner: Provider | by: 2026-08-01   (custom keyword — invent these freely, §2c; mix with reserved ones)
  clause: Limitation of Liability | cap: 3 months fees | excludes: indirect damages
  deadline: First milestone | date: 2026-08-01 | consequence: 2% penalty
  section: 4. Execution
  approve: Legal review complete | by: Sara | role: Counsel | at: 2026-06-13
  approve: Executed for the company | by: Ahmed Al-Thani | role: CEO | at: 2026-06-13
  (pre-seal sign-offs are approve:. To SEAL: sealDocument(source,{signer}) → adds freeze:; for
   provable identity, @dotit/sign signDocumentCrypto() adds a cryptographic sign: line with
   hash:+key:+sig:. Never hand-write a hash.)

BANK / FINANCIAL REPORT (multi-page, running header/footer, KPIs, tables):

  page: | size: A4 | margin: 20mm
  header: Qatar National Bank — Quarterly Report
  footer: Q2 2026 · Page {{page}} of {{pages}}
  title: Quarterly Financial Report — Q2 2026
  toc:
  section: Performance
  metric: Net Income | value: 4.1B QAR | trend: up | target: 3.8B QAR
  metric: ROE | value: 18.6% | period: Q2
  break:
  section: Balance Sheet
  | Account | Q1 | Q2 |
  | Total Assets | 1,180B | 1,210B |
  | Deposits | 870B | 905B |
  metric: Capital Adequacy | value: 19.2%

ERP-GENERATED DOCS: author one template .it with {{placeholders}} + `each:` rows (see §5),
then parseAndMerge(template, data, { missing: "blank" }) per record. Server: issuePDF(template,
data, {signer}) for sealed PDF bytes. Validate output with validateDocumentSemantic(doc)
(general document linter: trust/template/workflow checks) before issuing.

## 11. Generation rules (follow these exactly)

1. ` | ` (space-pipe-space) is the property delimiter. For a LITERAL pipe in
   content or a value, escape it: `\|` (literal backslash: `\\`). Colons inside
   content/values need no escaping (`quote: He said: watch this` is fine).
2. One intent per line. No HTML, no Markdown headings (#), no raw CSS anywhere.
3. Prose bare, structure keyworded. Write ordinary narrative paragraphs as BARE prose
   (no `text:` needed) — it is cleaner and is the preferred style; keep `text:` for a
   paragraph that carries pipe-props (`| end:`, styling) or must literally start `Word:`.
   But never bury STRUCTURE in prose: a task is `task:`, a decision is `decision:`, money
   totals are `metric: Label | value: …`. When a concept is precise but has NO reserved
   keyword, INVENT a domain one (clause:, risk:, obligation:, sla:, warranty:) rather than
   flattening it into prose — custom keywords are idiomatic, typed, and queryable (§2c).
   Lean on the open vocabulary; don't confine output to the 41.
4. Dates: ISO format (2026-04-01 or 2026-04-01T09:00:00Z).
5. Tables: keep header and rows the same column count — pad a short row (e.g. a
   subtotal that doesn't fill every column) with an empty cell `| |`; `each:` goes on the header row.
6. Styled spans use `;` between props: [text]{ color: #c00; weight: bold }.
7. RTL documents: `meta: | dir: rtl` for the whole doc, or `dir: rtl` on a single
   block for a per-paragraph override (full RTL layout incl. tables/footers).
8. For templates, leave {{placeholders}} untouched; never resolve them yourself.
9. Don't invent style values — only the keys in §4. Don't invent trust hashes/sigs —
   only sealDocument()/computeDocumentHash() and @dotit/sign compute valid ones;
   verify with verifyDocument()/verifyCryptoSignatures()/verifyCertifications().
10. Receipts: `page: | size: 80mm auto | margin: 4mm`, 2–3 narrow table columns.

## 12. Minimal valid documents

Simplest:                       Agent workflow (every then:/else: must point at a real id:):
  title: Hello                    title: Onboarding Agent
  Hello, world                    step: Validate form | tool: validate | id: s1
                                  decision: Valid? | then: s2 | else: s3
Meeting notes:                    step: Provision | tool: infra | id: s2
  title: Sprint Planning          step: Notify failure | tool: email | id: s3
  done: OAuth shipped             result: Done | status: success
  task: Index builder | owner: A
  decision: Perf over features | by: Ahmed

TWO-SIDED ROWS + SPACING (1.1):
- `text: Customer Name | end: 2026-06-12` — content at line start, value at line
  end (invoice/report rows). RTL-aware automatically.
- `leading: 1.9` (line-height), `space-before: 6px`, `space-after: 24px` on any
  text-bearing block, or document-wide: `style: text | leading: 1.9`.
- RTL: any Arabic content makes the document RTL automatically; all layout
  mirrors via CSS logical properties. Write Arabic documents naturally.
ARABIC KEYWORDS (33 first-class localized keyword names — NOT aliases; write whole documents in
Arabic; keywords round-trip AS WRITTEN, so Arabic files stay Arabic and sealed hashes hold):
  عنوان=title  ملخص=summary  بيانات=meta  قسم=section  فرعي=sub  نص=text
  تنبيه=info  اقتباس=quote  استشهاد=cite  شيفرة=code  صورة=image  رابط=link
  مهمة=task  منجز=done  أعمدة=columns  صف=row  مؤشر=metric  تتبع=track
  اعتماد=approve  توقيع=sign  تجميد=freeze  تعديل=amendment  صفحة=page
  ترويسة=header  تذييل=footer  علامة=watermark  نمط=style  فاصل=break
  مهلة=deadline  جهة=contact  تواصل=contact  تعريف=def  مرجع=ref
Example (complete Arabic quotation):
  عنوان: عرض سعر — تأثيث المكتب الرئيسي
  ملخص: شركة الإتقان للتجارة — صالح حتى 2026-07-15
  قسم: البنود
  أعمدة: الوصف | الكمية | الإجمالي
  صف: كرسي مكتب تنفيذي | 12 | 10,200 QAR
  مؤشر: الإجمالي المستحق | value: 10,200 QAR
  مهمة: اعتماد العرض | owner: أحمد | due: 2026-06-20
Property KEYS stay English for now (owner:, due:, value:). Dates stay ISO.

BIDI (mixed Arabic/English): rendered values (table cells, dates, amounts,
owners) are direction-isolated automatically (dir="auto") — never reorder or
pad mixed-language values. Force document direction with `meta: | dir: rtl`.

## 13. Recommended keywords (best practice — NOT reserved, NOT required)

The keyword set is open, so for cross-document query interop, teams should converge on the
SAME word for the same concept. This is CONVENTION only — ignoring it never makes a document
non-conformant. Full curated list: RECOMMENDED-KEYWORDS.md
(https://github.com/intenttext/IntentText/blob/main/RECOMMENDED-KEYWORDS.md). Rule of thumb:

1. A reserved keyword exists? USE IT — don't reinvent: task: (not todo:/action:), decision:
   (not decided:), metric: (not kpi:/total:), info: (not note:/warning:), sign: (not
   signature:), cite: (not source:), headers:/row: (not table:/columns:).
2. No reserved keyword but a common concept? Reach for the conventional custom word before
   inventing a synonym:
   - contracts/legal: clause obligation warranty liability indemnity term party jurisdiction governing-law penalty
   - projects/delivery: milestone deliverable dependency blocker scope objective assumption constraint requirement risk
   - risk/compliance: risk control finding mitigation incident
   - service/finance: sla stakeholder payment expense fee
   - agent memory: fact entity preference belief question lesson skill
   - meetings: attendee agenda
3. Genuinely domain-specific? Invent freely — lowercase, hyphen-joined, singular
   (`delivery-term:`, not `delivery_term:` or `DeliveryTerms:`).
